cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out how many rational numbers between 0 and 1 can be written as a fraction in lowest terms such that when I multiply the numerator and the denominator, I get 20! Let me start by recalling what 20! is. 20 factorial is the product of all positive integers up to 20. I know that breaking down factorials into prime factors is often useful in these kinds of problems, so maybe I should start by prime factorizing 20!. Let me recall that the prime factors of n! can be found using Legendre's formula, which tells us how many times a prime number p divides into n! by summing the floor of n/p^k for k from 1 until p^k > n. So let me try to apply that to 20!. First, list all prime numbers up to 20. They are 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19. Now, let me compute the exponents for each prime in 20!. Starting with 2: - floor(20/2) = 10 - floor(20/4) = 5 - floor(20/8) = 2 - floor(20/16) = 1 - floor(20/32) = 0 Adding those up: 10 + 5 + 2 + 1 =
